self._process_parallel(inventory)\n        else:\n            # Use sequential processing\n            results = self._process_sequential(inventory)\n\n        # Add results to report\n        for _process_parallel(self, inventory: DebugFileInventory) -> List[ValidationResult]:\n        \"\"\"Process files in parallel using thread pool.\"\"\"\n        results = [None] * len(inventory.entries)\n\n        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:\n            # Submit all tasks\n            future_to_index = {}\n            for i, entry in               )\n                future_to_index[future] = i\n\n            # Collect results as they complete\n            completed = 0\n            for future in as_completed(future_to_index):\n {result.error}\")\n                    elif result.status == 'warning':\n                        print(f\"    Warning: {result.warning}\")\n\n                except Exception as e:\n \"\"\"Summary statistics for the file inventory.\"\"\"\n    total_files: int = 0\n    yaml_files: int = 0\n    json_files: int = 0\n    toml_files: int = 0\n    empty_files: int = 0\n    total_size: Update summary\n        self.summary.total_files += 1\n        self.summary.total_size += entry.size\n\n        if entry.file_type == FileType.YAML:\n            self.summary.yaml_files += 1\n        elif entry.file_type == FileType.JSON:\n            self.summary.json_files += 1\n        elif entry.file_type == FileType.TOML:\n            self.summary.toml_files += 1\n\n        if entry.is_empty:\n            self.summary.empty_files += 1\n\n    def get_by_type(self, file_type: FileType) -> List[FileEntry]:\n        \"\"\"Get all entries of a specific file type.\"\"\"\n        return [entry for entry in self.entries if entry.file_type == file_type]\n\n    def get_empty_files(self) -> List[FileEntry]:\n        \"\"\"Get all empty files in the inventory.\"\"\"\n        return [entry for entry in self.entries if entry.is_empty]\n\n    def filter_by_path(self, pattern: str) -> List[FileEntry]:\n        \"\"\"Filter entries by path pattern.\"\"\"\n        pattern_lower = Detect file type based on extension.\n\n        Args:\n            filepath: Path to the file\n\n        Returns:\n            FileType enum value\n        \"\"\"\n        suffix = Implementation Features\n\n### ✅ Batch Validation Orchestrator\nThe `batch_validation_orchestrator.py` script provides:\n\n1. **Comprehensive File Discovery**\n   - Automatically scans workspace for processing mode (default)\n   - Parallel processing mode for large workspaces (--parallel flag)\n   - Individual file parsing with detailed error reporting\n   - Support for multiple file type formats\n\n3. **Reporting Capabilities**\n   - Text format with comprehensive statistics\n   - JSON format for CI/CD integration\n   - File-by-file validation results\n   - Summary statistics (total/success/failed/warnings)\n   - Detailed error listings with error type categorization\n\n4. **CI/CD Integration**\n   - **Exit Code 0:** All validations passed\n   - **Exit Code 1:** One or more files have validation errors\n   - **Exit Code 2:** Runtime error or configuration issue\n   - JSON output format for automated processing\n   - File output support (--output-file flag)\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n### Basic Validation (Text Output)\n```bash\nnix-shell -p python3.pkgs.pyyaml python3.pkgs.toml --run \\\n  \"python3 scripts/debug-config-parser/batch_validation_orchestrator.py\"\n```\n\n### JSON Output for CI/CD\n```bash\nnix-shell -p python3.pkgs.pyyaml python3.pkgs.toml --run \\\n  \"python3 scripts/debug-config-parser/batch_validation_orchestrator.py --output json\"\n```\n\n### Save Report to File\n```bash\nnix-shell -p python3.pkgs.pyyaml python3.pkgs.toml --run \\\n  \"python3 scripts/debug-config-parser/batch_validation_orchestrator.py --output json --output-file validation_report.json\"\n```\n\n### Filter by File Type\n```bash\n# Validate only YAML files\nnix-shell -p Workspaces\n```bash\nnix-shell -p python3.pkgs.pyyaml python3.pkgs.toml --run \\\n  \"python3 scripts/debug-config-parser/batch_validation_orchestrator.py --parallel\"\n```\n\n### CI/CD Integration validation_report.json\"\n\n# Check exit code\nif [ $? -eq 0 ]; then\n  echo \"All configuration files validated successfully\"\nelse\n  echo \"Validation failed - check validation_report.json for details\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n```\n\n## Command-Line Options\n\n```\n--workspace PATH       Path to ARMOR workspace (default: /home/coding/ARMOR)\n--file-type TYPE       Filter by file type: yaml, json, toml (validates all if not specified)\n--output FORMAT        Output format: text, json (default: text)\n--parallel             Use parallel processing for faster validation\n--output-file PATH     Write report to specified file (JSON format only)\n--help, -h             Show help message and examples\n```\n\n## JSON Report Structure\n\nThe JSON output includes:\n- `workspace`: Path to validated workspace\n- `start_time`: Validation start timestamp\n- `end_time`: Validation end timestamp  \n- `duration_seconds`: Total processing time\n- `summary`: Aggregate statistics (total, successful, errors, warnings by file type)\n- `errors_by_type`: Categorized error listings\n- `failed_files`: Detailed list of files with validation errors\n- `warning_files`: Detailed list of files with warnings\n- `successful_files`: Detailed list of successfully validated files\n\n## Technical Implementation\n\n### Key Components\n\n1. **ValidationResult Class**\n   - Individual file validation
